dto.creator: slochau

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue:
-->

Volume steps


In this test, step response after a large volume change is tested. First, a patient is ventilated with a set target volume. Then, a change in target volume(VTend) is introduced, which should be immediately applied by the ventilator. Due to calculation for tube volume compensation, 2 to 3 breathes (depending on the step heigh) are necessary to adjust the volume measured by flow analyser. The test is passed if:
  1. The inspiration volume of the first 3, respectively 4, breaths remains between the former set volume value and the new set volume value, including their tolerances
  2. The inspiration volume after the 3rd or 4th breath is within 20% of the set target volume for 20 seconds.

The sequence for this test is:

(ASL) -> (Setup) -> (Exec) -> (Evaluation) -> (CleanUp)

  • (ASL)-Block: Sets ASL values according to the product requirements.
  • (Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.
  • (Exec)-Block:
    1. Starts ventilation
    2. Waits for 45 seconds
    3. Sets the target value to VTend
  • (Evaluation)-Block:
    1. Asserts for 10 seconds (2 breaths) or 15 seconds (3 breaths), that Vti measured by the flow analyser does not over- respectively undershoot by more than 20%
    2. Asserts for 20 seconds (4 breaths), that Vti stays within the tolerance of the set target value.
  • (CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation
